Immediately cancels the reservation without applying cancellation policy fees. The slot is freed for other bookings. This is a hard delete from the active schedule, though the record may be retained for reporting.

**When to use:** When restaurant staff need to remove a reservation (diner called to cancel, duplicate entry, error correction, or freeing a no-show slot).

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token. Intended for POS/host-stand integrations.

**Business logic:**
- Bypasses cancellation policy fee calculations (staff authority)
- The freed slot becomes immediately available in availability queries
- Triggers `reservation.cancelled` webhook event
- The reservation record is marked as cancelled, not physically deleted (audit trail)
- Associated slot locks (if any) are also released

**Error scenarios:**
- 404: Reservation not found
- 422: Reservation is already in `completed` state and cannot be cancelled

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/inhouse/reservations/{id} (use state='cancelled' for soft-cancel with policy enforcement).

get.description:
List guest profiles for a restaurant with optional search/filter parameters.

Returns guest records with contact info (tier-gated), tags, notes, and metadata. Supports filtering by tag, archive status, and text search on name/email.

**When to use:** Building guest directories, searching for guests during reservation creation, CRM dashboards, or syncing guest data to external marketing systems.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token. Copper tier returns anonymized profiles (IDs, tags, notes). Gold+ tier adds PII (name, email, phone).

**Business logic:**
- PII fields (first_name, last_name, email, phone) are only populated for Gold/Platinum tier clients
- Copper tier clients receive all other fields (tags, notes, visit history) without PII
- The `forgotten` flag indicates GDPR-erased profiles (only the ID remains)
- `archived` guests are hidden from default listings (pass `include_archived=true` to include them)
- Supports pagination via `limit`/`offset`
- Text search matches against name, email, and phone (Gold+ only)
- Tag filtering returns guests who have ALL specified tags (AND logic)

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests (create new guest), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/tags (manage guest tags).

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Filter guests updated after this ISO timestamp

post.description:
Create a new guest profile in the restaurant's CRM.

Adds a guest record with optional contact info, notes, and metadata. Returns the created guest with a generated UUID.

**When to use:** When a new diner makes their first reservation at the restaurant, during data imports from external CRM systems, or when host-stand staff manually add a walk-in guest.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token. Gold+ tier required to set PII fields (name, email, phone).

**Business logic:**
- Guest IDs are UUIDs generated server-side
- The `gpid` (global person ID) is optional — use it to link this profile to the same person's profiles at other restaurants in the tenant
- Duplicate detection is NOT automatic; check existing guests before creating to avoid duplicates
- PII fields can only be set by Gold+ tier clients (copper clients can create anonymous profiles)
- The guest is immediately available for linking to reservations
- Notes fields (notes, notes_special_relationship, notes_food_and_drink, notes_seating) are free-text for staff use

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Validation error (invalid email format, missing required fields)
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant, or attempting to set PII with copper tier

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests (list/search guests), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/tags (add tags to new guest).

email_optin.description: Whether the guest opted into email marketing

tags.description: Guest tags (e.g., VIP, Regular, Birthday)

sms_opt_in.description: Whether the guest opted into SMS notifications

put.description:
Set the complete list of tags for a guest profile, replacing any existing tags.

Accepts an array of tag IDs. Tags must be pre-created via the restaurant's tag management endpoints before they can be applied to guests.

**When to use:** Categorizing guests (VIP, regular, food blogger, allergies, preferences), updating tag assignments after staff review, or syncing tags from an external system.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- This is a full replacement operation — the provided array completely replaces existing tags
- To add a tag without removing others, first GET the guest's current tags, add the new one, then PUT
- Tag IDs must reference existing tags (created via PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags/{tag_id})
- Invalid tag IDs are silently ignored (no error, but the tag won't appear on the profile)
- Empty array removes all tags from the guest

**Error scenarios:**
- 404: Guest not found

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags (list available tags), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags/{tag_id} (create/update a tag definition).

Set loyalty program memberships and point balances for a guest profile.

Accepts a structured loyalty data object containing program memberships, tiers, point balances, and enrollment dates.

**When to use:** Syncing loyalty data from external loyalty platforms, recording VIP/rewards tier changes, or tracking point accumulation across visits.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Full replacement semantics — the provided object completely replaces existing loyalty data
- Supports multiple loyalty program memberships per guest
- Each membership can track: program name, tier/level, point balance, enrollment date, expiry date
- Loyalty data is informational and does not automatically trigger discounts or special treatment
- Use in conjunction with tags to mark loyalty-tier guests for staff recognition

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid data format
- 404: Guest not found

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/insights (related guest data), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/tags (tag as VIP/loyalty member).

Set computed insights and analytics data for a guest profile.

Accepts a structured insights object containing visit statistics, spend patterns, preferences, and behavioral data.

**When to use:** Pushing computed analytics from external systems, recording visit frequency and spend data, or storing preference insights derived from order history.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Full replacement semantics — provided object replaces existing insights
- Insights are typically computed externally (from POS data, reservation history, or analytics pipelines)
- Common insight fields: total_visits, average_spend, last_visit_date, favorite_dishes, preferred_dining_area
- This is a flexible key-value structure — you can store any JSON-serializable insights
- Insights are displayed to host-stand staff to inform service personalization

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid JSON structure
- 404: Guest not found

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/loyalties (loyalty data), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/properties (custom properties).

Set custom key-value properties on a guest profile for flexible data storage.

Accepts a structured properties object for storing arbitrary guest metadata that doesn't fit into predefined fields.

**When to use:** Storing custom CRM data (preferred table, dietary restrictions, seating preferences, occasion history), integration-specific metadata, or any structured data that varies by restaurant.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Full replacement semantics — provided object replaces all existing custom properties
- Properties are a flexible JSON object — define your own schema per restaurant
- Common uses: dietary_restrictions, preferred_table, celebration_dates, communication_preferences
- Maximum payload size: 16KB per guest
- Properties are visible to staff in host-stand/CRM views
- No server-side validation of property keys or values (schema is caller-defined)

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Payload too large (>16KB) or invalid JSON
- 404: Guest not found

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/insights (analytics data), POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests (initial guest creation with properties).

get.description:
Retrieve the profile photo for a specific guest.

Returns the guest's photo as a base64 data URI, or 404 if no photo is set. Photos are used for host-stand recognition.

**When to use:** Displaying guest photos in host-stand/CRM interfaces, verifying guest identity on arrival, or syncing photos to external systems.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Photos are stored as base64-encoded data URIs (data:image/jpeg;base64,...)
- Returns the photo directly in the response body
- No photo set returns 404 (not an empty response)
- Photos are typically uploaded by restaurant staff, not by diners

**Error scenarios:**
- 404: Guest not found or guest has no photo set

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/photos (upload photo), DELETE /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/photos (remove photo).

put.description:
Upload or replace the profile photo for a guest.

Accepts a base64-encoded image (JPEG or PNG) as a data URI. The photo is used for host-stand recognition and CRM displays.

**When to use:** When staff want to add a recognizable photo of a regular guest, during guest profile setup, or syncing photos from an external system.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Accepts data URIs in format: `data:image/{jpeg|png};base64,{encoded_data}`
- Maximum file size: 500KB (after base64 encoding)
- Replaces any existing photo (only one photo per guest)
- Supported formats: JPEG, PNG
- No automatic face detection or validation — any image is accepted
- The photo is immediately available via the GET endpoint

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid data URI format, unsupported image type, or exceeds 500KB limit
- 404: Guest not found

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/photos (retrieve photo), DELETE /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/photos (remove photo).

delete.description:
Remove the profile photo from a guest record.

Deletes the stored photo permanently. Subsequent GET requests for this guest's photo will return 404.

**When to use:** Guest requests photo removal (privacy), photo is outdated, or clearing incorrect photo assignments.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Permanent deletion — the photo data is removed, not archived
- Idempotent: deleting a non-existent photo returns success (not 404)
- Does not affect any other guest profile data

**Error scenarios:**
- 404: Guest not found

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/photos (upload new photo).

get.description:
List all guest tag definitions available at a restaurant.

Returns the tag catalog: each tag has an ID, display name, color, and category. Tags are applied to guest profiles for categorization and filtering.

**When to use:** Populating tag selection UIs in CRM interfaces, validating tag IDs before applying them to guests, or syncing the tag catalog to external systems.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Tags are restaurant-scoped — each restaurant has its own tag catalog
- Returns all tags including inactive ones
- Tags have: id, name, color (hex), category (optional grouping)
- Common categories: dietary, preferences, status, marketing
- The tag list is typically small (10-50 tags per restaurant)

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags/{tag_id} (create/update tag), DELETE /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags/{tag_id} (delete tag), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guest_id}/tags (apply tags to guest).

put.description:
Create a new tag or update an existing tag definition for a restaurant.

Uses upsert semantics: if the tag_id exists, its properties are updated; if it doesn't exist, a new tag is created with that ID.

**When to use:** Setting up the tag catalog for a new restaurant, adding new categorization options, or updating tag display properties (name, color).

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Tag IDs are caller-defined (you choose the ID, not the server)
- Updating a tag's name/color does not affect existing guest-tag associations
- Tags can be created in bulk by making multiple upsert calls
- Tag properties: name (display label), color (hex code for UI), category (optional grouping)
- There is no limit on the number of tags per restaurant

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid tag data (missing name, invalid color format)
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags (list all tags), DELETE /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags/{tag_id} (delete tag).

delete.description:
Delete a tag definition from a restaurant's catalog.

Removes the tag from the catalog and disassociates it from all guest profiles that currently have it. This is a cascading delete.

**When to use:** Removing obsolete or incorrect tag definitions, cleaning up the tag catalog, or replacing a tag with a new one.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Cascading: the tag is removed from all guest profiles that have it
- The deletion is permanent — recreating a tag with the same ID does not restore guest associations
- Deleting a non-existent tag returns success (idempotent)

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags (list tags), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/tags/{tag_id} (create replacement tag).

get.description:
List all menus configured for a restaurant with summary metadata.

Returns menu records with ID, name, description, ordinal (display order), and group count. Does not include full item details — use the get-by-ID endpoint for complete menu content.

**When to use:** Displaying a menu index or navigation (lunch, dinner, drinks, desserts), checking what menus exist before a sync operation, or building a menu management UI.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Returns menus ordered by their `ordinal` field (lowest first)
- Includes metadata only — not full item listings (performance optimization for restaurants with large menus)
- Empty restaurants (no menus) return an empty array, not 404

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/menus/{menu_id} (full menu with items), POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/menus (push/replace all menus).

200.description: All menus with groups and items

post.description:
Push a complete menu set for a restaurant, replacing all existing menus.

Accepts an array of menu payloads with groups and items. This is a full replacement operation — all existing menus are deleted and replaced with the provided set.

**When to use:** Full menu syncs from POS systems, bulk menu imports, or menu management systems that maintain the canonical menu externally and push updates to FreeRez.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- FULL REPLACEMENT: all existing menus, groups, and items are deleted before inserting the new set
- Menus, groups, and items are nested: Menu → Groups → Items
- Each item includes: name, description, price (smallest denomination), currency, dietary flags
- The `ordinal` field on menus and groups controls display ordering
- Supports multiple currencies across items (e.g., drinks in local currency, tasting menu in USD)
- Maximum payload: 1MB per request
- Returns the created menu set with server-generated IDs

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid menu structure, missing required fields, or payload too large
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/menus (list created menus), GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/menus/{menu_id} (verify specific menu).

get.description:
Retrieve a specific menu with full content including all groups and items.

Returns the complete menu structure: menu metadata, groups (categories/sections), and all items within each group with pricing and descriptions.

**When to use:** Displaying a full menu on a restaurant's booking page, showing menu details during the reservation flow, or verifying menu content after a sync.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Returns the hierarchical structure: menu → groups → items
- Items include: name, description, price, currency, dietary indicators, and availability
- Groups represent menu sections (e.g., Starters, Mains, Desserts, Wines)
- Prices are in the smallest currency denomination
- The `ordinal` field on items and groups indicates display order

**Error scenarios:**
- 404: Menu ID not found for this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/menus (list all menus), POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/menus (replace menus).

List diner reviews for a restaurant with optional filtering and pagination.

Returns review records including ratings, text, diner context, moderation status, and any management replies. Ordered by submission date (newest first).

**When to use:** Displaying reviews on a restaurant's public profile, monitoring review activity, exporting reviews for analysis, or building a review management dashboard.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Reviews include overall rating and sub-ratings (food, service, ambiance, value) on a 1-5 scale
- Only reviews in `approved` moderation state are shown to diners (but the API returns all states for management)
- The `DinerIsVip` flag indicates reviews from high-engagement diners
- Review photos are included in the `Photos` array when present
- Pagination via `limit`/`offset`; default limit is 20
- Can filter by: rating range, date range, moderation state, featured status

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/reviews/summary (aggregate statistics), POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/reviews/{review_id}/replies (respond to a review).

Reviews.ReviewText: Absolutely incredible experience. The dry-aged porterhouse was perfection.

post.description:
Submit a new diner review for a restaurant.

Creates a review record with ratings, optional text commentary, and diner context. Reviews are automatically approved on creation.

**When to use:** Submitting diner feedback after a completed reservation, syncing reviews from an external source, or migrating review data from another platform.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Business logic:**
- Overall rating (1-5) is required; sub-ratings (food, service, ambience, value, noise) are optional
- A unique ReviewId is auto-generated in the format `OT-{rid}-{timestamp}`
- Reviews are created with `APPROVED` moderation state by default
- DinerInitials are auto-derived from CustomerNickname if not provided
- Categories are optional tag arrays for categorizing the dining experience

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing required fields or invalid rating values
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/reviews (list reviews), GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/reviews/summary (aggregate statistics).

example.ReviewText: An absolutely magical evening. Every course was perfection.

get.description:
Retrieve aggregate review statistics for a restaurant including average ratings, total count, and rating distribution.

Returns a statistical summary suitable for displaying on restaurant profile pages: overall average, category averages, total review count, and a histogram of rating distribution.

**When to use:** Displaying star ratings on restaurant cards/profiles, showing rating breakdowns, comparing performance across categories, or feeding analytics dashboards.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Averages are calculated across all approved reviews only (pending/rejected excluded)
- Sub-ratings (food, service, ambiance, value) are averaged independently
- The distribution histogram shows count of reviews at each star level (1-5)
- The `total` count reflects approved reviews only
- If the restaurant has zero approved reviews, averages are null (not zero)
- Summary is recalculated in real-time (not cached), so it's always current

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/reviews (individual review listing).

post.description:
Post a management reply to a specific diner review.

Allows the restaurant to respond publicly to a review. Each review can have one management reply; subsequent calls update the existing reply.

**When to use:** Responding to diner feedback (both positive and negative), addressing complaints publicly, thanking diners for positive reviews, or correcting factual inaccuracies.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Each review can have at most one management reply
- Calling this endpoint again on the same review replaces the previous reply
- Replies are immediately visible to diners viewing the review
- Reply text should be professional — it's publicly displayed
- Maximum reply length: 2,000 characters
- Replies cannot be made on reviews in `pending` moderation state

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Reply text empty or exceeds 2,000 characters
- 404: Review not found
- 422: Review is in pending moderation state (cannot reply until approved/rejected)

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/reviews (find review IDs to reply to).

ReplyingToReplyId.description: ID of a previous reply to respond to

example.Message: Thank you so much for your kind words! We're thrilled you enjoyed the porterhouse. We look forward to welcoming you back soon.
example.Name: Management Team

example.Message: Thank you so much for your kind words!
example.Name: Management Team

get.description:
Retrieve the current POS integration status for a restaurant.

Returns the connection state, configuration details, last sync timestamp, and any error information for the restaurant's POS integration.

**When to use:** Monitoring POS connectivity health, displaying integration status in dashboards, or diagnosing ticket push failures.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Status values: `connected` (healthy), `disconnected` (not configured), `error` (configured but failing)
- Includes last successful sync timestamp and error details if applicable
- POS configuration includes: system type, venue ID, and sync frequency
- A `disconnected` status means no POS has been configured for this restaurant

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found

**Related endpoints:** PATCH /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos (update status/config), GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos/tickets (list tickets), POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos/tickets (push ticket).

patch.description:
Update the POS integration status or configuration for a restaurant.

Supports partial updates via PATCH — only include the fields you want to change. Used to register a POS connection, update configuration, or report connection state changes.

**When to use:** Initial POS system registration, reporting connection state changes (connected/error), updating venue IDs or sync settings, or clearing error states.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- PATCH semantics: only provided fields are updated
- Setting status to `connected` clears any previous error details
- Setting status to `error` should include an error message for diagnostics
- Configuration changes (venue ID, system type) do not require disconnecting first
- Timestamp fields are automatically updated on any modification

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid status value or configuration format
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos (read current status).

get.description:
List POS tickets (completed checks/bills) associated with reservations at a restaurant.

Returns ticket records including items ordered, amounts, payment details, and the linked reservation ID. Supports filtering by date range.

**When to use:** Revenue reporting, guest spend analysis, reconciling POS data with reservation records, or displaying order history on guest profiles.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Tickets are linked to reservations via the reservation ID
- Each ticket includes: items, subtotal, tax, tip, total, payment method, server, and timestamps
- Currency amounts are in the smallest denomination
- Supports date range filtering and pagination
- Tickets without a valid reservation link are still stored but may have null reservation references

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos/tickets (push new ticket), GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos (check POS status first).

parameters.name: updated_from
parameters.description: Filter tickets updated after this time

parameters.name: updated_to
parameters.description: Filter tickets updated before this time

post.description:
Push a completed POS ticket (check/bill) and link it to a reservation.

Accepts ticket data from the POS system and stores it linked to the specified reservation. Automatically updates the reservation's `pos_data` field and may enrich guest insights.

**When to use:** After a diner pays their bill in the POS system, push the ticket data to FreeRez for spend tracking, guest history enrichment, and reporting.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- The ticket is linked to a reservation by reservation ID (provided in the request body)
- If the reservation ID is valid, the reservation's `pos_data` field is updated with a reference
- Ticket data includes: items (name, quantity, price), subtotal, tax, tip, total, payment method
- Duplicate ticket pushes (same ticket ID) are idempotent — the record is updated, not duplicated
- Currency amounts must be in the smallest denomination matching the restaurant's configured currency
- Guest insights may be automatically updated if the reservation has a linked guest profile

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid ticket data structure or missing required fields
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant
- 404: Referenced reservation not found (ticket is still stored but unlinked)

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos/tickets (verify pushed ticket), GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/pos (check POS connectivity).

Submit a private dining or special event inquiry for a restaurant.

Captures lead information for the restaurant's events team to review and follow up on. Returns a confirmation reference for the inquirer.

**When to use:** When a diner submits a private event inquiry form, requesting information about buyouts, private rooms, large group bookings, or catered events.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Leads are one-way: submitted and delivered to the restaurant's events team
- No slot lock or availability check is required — private dining is handled manually
- Lead data includes: contact info (name, email, phone), event details (date preferences, guest count, budget range, occasion type), and special requirements (dietary, AV, floral)
- The restaurant is notified via email and the lead appears in their admin dashboard
- Returns a confirmation ID for the submitter to reference in follow-up
- There is no update or retrieval endpoint — leads are managed in the restaurant's internal tools

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing required contact information or invalid data formats
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found or does not have private dining enabled

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid} (check if restaurant has private dining in profile).

201.description: Lead created - returns lead ID as plain string

get.description:
List all webhook subscriptions for a restaurant.

Returns webhook records including URL, subscribed events, active status, secret (masked), and timestamps.

**When to use:** Auditing webhook configuration, building a webhook management UI, verifying subscription state, or troubleshooting delivery issues.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Returns all webhooks regardless of active/inactive status
- The `secret` field is masked in list responses (only shown in full at creation time)
- The `events` array shows which event types trigger this webhook
- Inactive webhooks are not called but retain their configuration for reactivation
- Results are scoped to the client's `partner_id` if set (partners only see their own webhooks)

**Error scenarios:**
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks (create new subscription), GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (details of specific webhook).

post.description:
Create a new webhook subscription for real-time event delivery to your HTTPS endpoint.

Registers a URL to receive POST requests whenever specified events occur at the restaurant. Returns the webhook configuration including the signing secret.

**When to use:** Setting up real-time integrations (sync reservations to external calendar, trigger notifications on booking, feed analytics pipelines), or during initial integration onboarding.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- The `url` must be HTTPS (HTTP endpoints are rejected)
- A `secret` is generated server-side for HMAC-SHA256 payload signing — store it securely
- The secret is only returned in the creation response; it cannot be retrieved later
- Available events: `reservation.created`, `reservation.updated`, `reservation.cancelled`, `reservation.seated`, `reservation.completed`, `reservation.noshow`, `guest.created`, `guest.updated`
- Subscribe to multiple events by passing an array
- The webhook is created in `active: true` state and begins receiving events immediately
- A test event is NOT automatically sent — use a real operation to verify
- The `partner_id` is automatically set from the client's configuration

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid URL (not HTTPS), empty events array, or malformed request
- 403: Client does not have access to this restaurant
- 409: A webhook with the same URL already exists for this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks (list all), PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (update), DELETE /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (remove).

url.description: Endpoint URL to receive webhook POSTs

secret.description: Shared secret for HMAC signature verification

get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific webhook subscription by ID.

Returns the webhook configuration including URL, events, active status, and delivery statistics. The signing secret is masked.

**When to use:** Checking a specific webhook's configuration, verifying active status, debugging delivery issues, or confirming event subscriptions.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- The `secret` field is masked (only first/last 4 characters shown)
- May include delivery statistics: last delivery timestamp, success/failure counts
- Inactive webhooks retain their configuration for reactivation

**Error scenarios:**
- 404: Webhook ID not found for this restaurant

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (update), DELETE /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (delete).

put.description:
Update a webhook subscription's configuration including URL, events, and active status.

Accepts partial updates — only include fields you want to change. Commonly used to add/remove event subscriptions or reactivate a deactivated webhook.

**When to use:** Adding new event types to an existing subscription, changing the delivery URL, reactivating a webhook after fixing delivery issues, or temporarily disabling a webhook.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Partial update: only provided fields are modified
- Setting `active: true` reactivates a deactivated webhook (consecutive failure counter resets)
- Changing the URL does not regenerate the signing secret
- The events array is fully replaced when provided (not merged with existing events)
- Changes take effect immediately for the next event delivery
- Cannot update the `secret` — delete and recreate the webhook if a new secret is needed

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid URL or empty events array
- 404: Webhook not found

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (read current state), DELETE /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (delete instead).

delete.description:
Permanently delete a webhook subscription, stopping all future event deliveries.

Removes the webhook configuration entirely. Any in-flight deliveries may still complete, but no new events will be sent to this URL.

**When to use:** Integration decommissioning, replacing a webhook with a new configuration (delete + create), or cleaning up unused subscriptions.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token.

**Business logic:**
- Deletion is permanent — the webhook ID and secret cannot be recovered
- In-flight deliveries at the time of deletion may still arrive at the endpoint
- Any pending retries for failed deliveries are cancelled
- Deleting an already-deleted webhook returns success (idempotent)
- Consider setting `active: false` via update instead if you may want to reactivate later

**Error scenarios:**
- 404: Webhook not found (may have already been deleted)

**Related endpoints:** PUT /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks/{webhook_id} (use `active: false` for soft-disable), POST /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/webhooks (create new subscription).

get.description:
Redirect the user to the OAuth provider's consent screen to authorize a connection between a restaurant and a third-party service.

This is a browser-flow endpoint called from the FreeRez dashboard. It creates or updates a pending integration record, generates a CSRF nonce, and redirects the user to the provider's authorization URL.

**When to use:** When a restaurant operator clicks "Connect" on an integration card in the dashboard settings page.

**Required permissions:** Active user session (cookie-based authentication). This endpoint does NOT use Bearer token auth.

**Business logic:**
- The `restaurantId` query parameter identifies which restaurant to connect
- If an integration record already exists for this restaurant+provider, it is reset to `pending` status with a new nonce
- If no record exists, a new one is created in `pending` status
- The `state` parameter sent to the OAuth provider encodes: restaurantId, providerId, userId, nonce, and returnUrl
- The `returnUrl` defaults to `/dashboard/settings` if not provided
- After authorization, the provider redirects back to the callback endpoint

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing `restaurantId` query parameter
- 401: No active user session
- 404: Unknown provider identifier
- 503: Provider not configured (missing client ID/secret environment variables)

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/integrations/{provider}/callback (handles the OAuth redirect), POST /api/v1/integrations/{provider}/disconnect (removes the connection).

parameters.name: restaurantId
parameters.description: The restaurant ID to connect this integration to

parameters.name: returnUrl
parameters.description: URL to redirect back to after OAuth completes. Defaults to `/dashboard/settings`.

302.description: Redirect to the OAuth provider's consent screen. The `Location` header contains the provider's authorization URL with `client_id`, `redirect_uri`, `scope`, `state`, and `response_type=code` parameters.

get.description:
Process the OAuth provider's redirect after the user authorizes (or denies) the connection request.

This endpoint receives the authorization code from the provider, validates the state parameter (CSRF nonce, user ID, provider ID), exchanges the code for access/refresh tokens, and stores them in the integration record.

**When to use:** This endpoint is called automatically by the OAuth provider's redirect. It should never be called directly by client code.

**Required permissions:** Active user session (cookie-based authentication). The session user must match the user who initiated the flow.

**Business logic:**
- The `state` parameter is decoded and validated against the stored nonce in the pending integration record
- If the user denies authorization, the `error` query parameter is set and the user is redirected to `/dashboard/settings?error=oauth_denied`
- The authorization code is exchanged for tokens using the provider's token endpoint
- Tokens are encrypted and stored in the integration record, which transitions from `pending` to `active`
- On success, redirects to the `returnUrl` from the original connect request with `?connected={provider}` appended
- On token exchange failure, redirects to `/dashboard/settings?error=oauth_failed`

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing `code` or `state` parameter, invalid state, no pending integration found, invalid nonce
- 403: Session user does not match the user who initiated the OAuth flow
- 404: Unknown provider
- 503: Provider not configured

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/integrations/{provider}/connect (initiates the flow that leads here).

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Authorization code returned by the OAuth provider. Present on successful authorization.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque state string encoding CSRF nonce, restaurant ID, user ID, and return URL. Must match the value sent during the connect step.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: Error code from the OAuth provider if the user denied the request (e.g., `access_denied`).

302.description: Redirect back to the FreeRez dashboard. On success: `{returnUrl}?connected={provider}`. On denial: `/dashboard/settings?error=oauth_denied`. On failure: `/dashboard/settings?error=oauth_failed`.

post.description:
Disconnect a third-party integration from a restaurant, revoking OAuth tokens and clearing stored credentials.

This endpoint revokes the access token with the provider (best-effort), clears all stored tokens and metadata, and sets the integration status to `inactive`.

**When to use:** When a restaurant operator clicks "Disconnect" on an active integration in the dashboard settings page.

**Required permissions:** Active user session (cookie-based authentication).

**Business logic:**
- Finds the integration record for the given restaurant+provider combination
- Attempts to revoke the access token with the OAuth provider (failure is silently ignored)
- Clears `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, `tokenExpiresAt`, `partnerIdentifier`, and `metadata`
- Sets integration status to `inactive`
- Returns `{ "disconnected": true }` on success

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing `restaurantId` in request body
- 401: No active user session
- 404: Unknown provider, or no integration found for this restaurant+provider

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/integrations/{provider}/connect (re-connect after disconnecting).

restaurantId.description: The restaurant ID to disconnect this integration from

404.description: Unknown provider or integration not found

post.description:
Trigger a full synchronization of reviews from the restaurant's linked Google Business Profile into the FreeRez review system.

Fetches all reviews from the Google My Business API (paginated), and upserts them into the FreeRez reviews table. New reviews are created with `source: google-my-business` and a `gmb-` prefixed review ID. Existing reviews are updated if their `updateTime` has changed. Review replies from Google are also imported.

**When to use:** On-demand sync from the dashboard, or periodically via a cron job to keep reviews fresh.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Known issue:** This endpoint calls `requireAuthorizedRid` which reads `rid` from the URL path parameters, but the route path does not include a `{rid}` segment. Until this is fixed, this endpoint will return 400. The intended behavior is documented here for when the route is corrected.

**Business logic:**
- Looks up the active `google-my-business` integration for the restaurant
- Automatically refreshes the OAuth access token if expired
- Fetches all Google reviews in pages of 50
- For each review: creates a new FreeRez review (with `gmb-` prefix) or updates an existing one if `updateTime` changed
- Google star ratings are mapped: ONE=1, TWO=2, THREE=3, FOUR=4, FIVE=5
- Review replies are imported and linked to their parent review
- Updates `lastReviewSyncAt` timestamp in integration metadata
- Returns sync statistics: count of synced, created, updated, and any per-review errors

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing or invalid restaurant ID, no Google location linked
- 401: Invalid Bearer token
- 403: Client lacks access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found, Google Business Profile not connected
- 503: Integration not configured (missing Google OAuth credentials)

**Related endpoints:** POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/reviews/{review_id}/reply (reply to a synced review), POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/webhook (automated sync via Pub/Sub).

200.description: Sync completed. Returns statistics about the sync operation.

synced.description: Total number of reviews successfully processed

created.description: Number of new reviews created in FreeRez

errors.description: Per-review error messages for reviews that failed to sync

post.description:
Post a management reply to a Google review through the Google My Business API. The reply is published on the restaurant's Google Business Profile and visible to all Google users.

The review must have been synced from Google (identified by the `gmb-` prefix on its review ID). The reply is sent directly to Google's API and the response includes Google's confirmation timestamp.

**When to use:** When a restaurant operator wants to respond to a Google review from within the FreeRez dashboard.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Known issue:** This endpoint calls `requireAuthorizedRid` which reads `rid` from the URL path parameters, but the route path does not include a `{rid}` segment. Until this is fixed, this endpoint will return 400. The intended behavior is documented here for when the route is corrected.

**Business logic:**
- Validates the review exists and belongs to the restaurant
- Verifies the review originated from Google (has `gmb-` prefixed review ID)
- Looks up the active Google Business Profile integration
- Refreshes the OAuth token if needed
- Sends the reply via Google's `PUT .../reviews/{reviewId}/reply` API
- Returns the reply message, Google's update timestamp, and the FreeRez review ID

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing or invalid restaurant ID, missing `Message` in body, review is not from Google, no location linked
- 401: Invalid Bearer token
- 403: Client lacks access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found, review not found, Google Business Profile not connected
- 503: Integration not configured

**Related endpoints:** POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/reviews/sync (sync reviews before replying), GET /api/v1/restaurants/{rid}/reviews (list all reviews including synced Google reviews).

Message.description: The reply text to post on Google. This will be publicly visible.

example.Message: Thank you for your kind words! We look forward to welcoming you back soon.

Message.description: The reply text that was posted

GoogleUpdateTime.description: Timestamp from Google confirming when the reply was published

ReviewId.description: The FreeRez review UUID that was replied to

example.Message: Thank you for your kind words! We look forward to welcoming you back soon.

get.description:
Compare the restaurant's opening hours in FreeRez with the hours published on their Google Business Profile listing.

Returns a day-by-day comparison showing whether hours match and, for any mismatches, the specific time slots from each source.

**When to use:** Before pushing or pulling hours, to preview what would change. Useful for detecting drift between systems.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Known issue:** This endpoint calls `requireAuthorizedRid` which reads `rid` from the URL path parameters, but the route path does not include a `{rid}` segment. Until this is fixed, this endpoint will return 400. The intended behavior is documented here for when the route is corrected.

**Business logic:**
- Fetches the restaurant's `openingTimes` from the FreeRez database
- Fetches the location's `regularHours` from the Google Business Information API
- Compares each day of the week (Monday through Sunday)
- Time slots are normalized to `HH:MM` format before comparison
- Returns `matches: true` if all days are identical, or `matches: false` with a `diffs` array detailing each mismatched day
- A day with no hours in one system and hours in the other counts as a mismatch

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing or invalid restaurant ID, no Google location linked
- 401: Invalid Bearer token
- 403: Client lacks access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found, Google Business Profile not connected
- 503: Integration not configured

**Related endpoints:** POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/hours/push (push FreeRez hours to Google), POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/hours/pull (pull Google hours into FreeRez).

matches.description: Whether all days of the week have identical hours in both systems

diffs.description: Array of day-by-day differences. Empty if `matches` is true.

day.description: Day of the week (e.g., Monday, Tuesday)

freerez.description: Time slots from FreeRez, or null if no hours configured

google.description: Time slots from Google, or null if no hours configured

post.description:
Push the restaurant's FreeRez opening hours to their Google Business Profile listing, overwriting Google's current hours.

Converts FreeRez's day-of-week time slot format into Google's `regularHours` period format and sends a PATCH request to the Google Business Information API.

**When to use:** After updating hours in FreeRez, to propagate the change to Google. Typically used after reviewing differences via the compare endpoint.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Known issue:** This endpoint calls `requireAuthorizedRid` which reads `rid` from the URL path parameters, but the route path does not include a `{rid}` segment. Until this is fixed, this endpoint will return 400. The intended behavior is documented here for when the route is corrected.

**Business logic:**
- Reads the restaurant's `openingTimes` from the FreeRez database
- Fails with 400 if no hours are configured in FreeRez
- Converts each day's time slots into Google's `TimePeriod` format (openDay, openTime, closeDay, closeTime)
- Sends a PATCH to Google's Business Information API with `updateMask=regularHours`
- Returns confirmation with the number of time periods pushed

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing or invalid restaurant ID, no hours configured in FreeRez, no Google location linked
- 401: Invalid Bearer token
- 403: Client lacks access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found, Google Business Profile not connected
- 503: Integration not configured

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/hours/compare (preview before pushing), POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/hours/pull (pull hours from Google instead).

periods.description: Number of time periods sent to Google

post.description:
Pull the restaurant's opening hours from their Google Business Profile listing and save them as the restaurant's FreeRez opening times, overwriting current FreeRez hours.

Fetches Google's `regularHours` and converts them into FreeRez's day-of-week time slot format.

**When to use:** When Google is the source of truth for hours, or during initial setup to import existing hours. Typically used after reviewing differences via the compare endpoint.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Known issue:** This endpoint calls `requireAuthorizedRid` which reads `rid` from the URL path parameters, but the route path does not include a `{rid}` segment. Until this is fixed, this endpoint will return 400. The intended behavior is documented here for when the route is corrected.

**Business logic:**
- Fetches `regularHours` from the Google Business Information API via `readMask=regularHours`
- Converts Google's `TimePeriod` format into FreeRez's `{ day: [{ start, end }] }` format
- Time slots are sorted by start time within each day
- Updates the restaurant's `openingTimes` field in the database
- Returns confirmation with the converted hours object

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing or invalid restaurant ID, no Google location linked
- 401: Invalid Bearer token
- 403: Client lacks access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found, Google Business Profile not connected, no hours found on Google listing
- 503: Integration not configured

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/hours/compare (preview before pulling), POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/hours/push (push hours to Google instead).

200.description: Hours pulled from Google and saved to FreeRez

hours.description: The converted opening times in FreeRez format, keyed by day of week

get.description:
List all Google Business accounts accessible by the connected OAuth credentials.

Used during initial setup to let the restaurant operator choose which Google Business account contains their restaurant location.

**When to use:** After completing the OAuth flow, as the first step in linking a specific Google location to the restaurant.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Known issue:** This endpoint calls `requireAuthorizedRid` which reads `rid` from the URL path parameters, but the route path does not include a `{rid}` segment. Until this is fixed, this endpoint will return 400. The intended behavior is documented here for when the route is corrected.

**Business logic:**
- Looks up the active Google Business Profile integration for the restaurant
- Refreshes the OAuth token if needed
- Calls Google's Account Management API (`/v1/accounts`)
- Returns the list of accounts with their names, display names, types, and roles

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing or invalid restaurant ID
- 401: Invalid Bearer token
- 403: Client lacks access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found, Google Business Profile not connected
- 503: Integration not configured

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/locations (list locations within an account).

List all locations within a Google Business account. Used during setup to let the operator select which Google location corresponds to their restaurant.

**When to use:** After selecting an account from the accounts endpoint, to choose the specific location to link.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Known issue:** This endpoint calls `requireAuthorizedRid` which reads `rid` from the URL path parameters, but the route path does not include a `{rid}` segment. Until this is fixed, this endpoint will return 400. The intended behavior is documented here for when the route is corrected.

**Business logic:**
- Requires the `account` query parameter specifying the Google account resource name
- Looks up the active Google Business Profile integration for the restaurant
- Refreshes the OAuth token if needed
- Calls Google's Business Information API with `readMask=name,title,storefrontAddress`
- Returns locations with their resource names, titles, and addresses

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Missing or invalid restaurant ID, missing `account` query parameter
- 401: Invalid Bearer token
- 403: Client lacks access to this restaurant
- 404: Restaurant not found, Google Business Profile not connected
- 503: Integration not configured

**Related endpoints:** GET /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/accounts (list accounts first, then list locations within the chosen account).

parameters.name: account
parameters.description: Google Business account resource name (e.g., `accounts/123456789`). Obtain from the accounts endpoint.

200.description: List of locations within the Google Business account

post.description:
Receive push notifications from Google Cloud Pub/Sub when changes occur on a linked Google Business Profile (e.g., new review posted).

This endpoint is registered as a Pub/Sub push subscription endpoint. When Google detects a change (such as a new review), it sends a Pub/Sub message to this endpoint, which triggers an automatic review sync for the affected location.

**When to use:** This endpoint is not called directly by API clients. It is configured as a Google Cloud Pub/Sub push endpoint during integration setup.

**Required permissions:** None (public endpoint). Optionally verified via the `GMB_PUBSUB_VERIFICATION_TOKEN` environment variable.

**Business logic:**
- Parses the Pub/Sub message envelope: `{ "message": { "data": "base64...", "attributes": { "token": "..." } } }`
- If `GMB_PUBSUB_VERIFICATION_TOKEN` is set, validates the `token` attribute against it
- Decodes the base64 `data` field to extract the `location` resource name
- Looks up the active integration matching that location's `partnerIdentifier`
- Triggers an async review sync using `waitUntil` (non-blocking)
- Always returns `{ "ok": true }` to acknowledge the message (even if no matching integration is found)
- Errors during sync are logged but do not cause a non-200 response (to prevent Pub/Sub retries for application errors)

**Error scenarios:**
- 400: Invalid JSON body, no message in body, invalid base64 data
- 403: Invalid verification token (when `GMB_PUBSUB_VERIFICATION_TOKEN` is configured)

**Related endpoints:** POST /api/v1/integrations/google-my-business/reviews/sync (manual sync alternative).

schema.description: Google Cloud Pub/Sub push message envelope

data.description: Base64-encoded JSON containing the notification payload. When decoded, contains a `location` field with the Google location resource name.

token.description: Verification token for authenticating Pub/Sub messages. Validated against `GMB_PUBSUB_VERIFICATION_TOKEN` env var if set.

200.description: Message acknowledged. Returns `{ "ok": true }` regardless of whether a sync was triggered.

get.description:
List all staff members for a restaurant, including pending invites.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Note:** This is a FreeRez-specific endpoint — OpenTable does not expose a staff management API.

post.description:
Invite a new team member to the restaurant. Creates a user account if one doesn't exist, generates an invite token, and optionally sends an invite email.

The invited member must accept the invitation at `/invite/{token}` to set their password and activate their account.

**Required permissions:** Valid Bearer token with access to the restaurant.

**Business logic:**
- Duplicate invites to the same email for the same restaurant return 409
- Invite tokens expire after 7 days
- The `Active` field is `false` until the invite is accepted
- If email notifications are configured, an invite email is sent automatically

Name.description: Display name. Defaults to email prefix if omitted.

409.description: Member already exists for this restaurant

put.description: Update a staff member's role or active status.

delete.description: Remove a staff member from the restaurant. The restaurant owner cannot be removed (returns 403).

get.description: List all saved floor plan layout presets for a restaurant.

200.description: List of layouts with active layout ID

post.description: Save the current floor plan as a named layout preset. If layout_data is omitted, the restaurant's current floor plan is used.

get.summary: Get layout details with full data

get.description: List all active server sections for a restaurant, optionally filtered by date.

color.description: Hex color for floor plan visualization

post.description: Create a new server section assigning a staff member to a set of tables for a specific date.

AdminKey.name: X-Admin-Key
AdminKey.description: Platform admin API key for administrative operations

Forbidden.description: Client does not have access to this restaurant (allowedRids restriction)

IntegrationNotConnected.description: Integration not connected for this restaurant

IntegrationNotConfigured.description: Integration provider not configured on this FreeRez instance (missing environment variables)

message.description: Human-readable error description suitable for logging or developer debugging. Not intended for display to end users.

code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic error handling (e.g., INVALID_TOKEN, SLOT_EXPIRED, CAPACITY_EXCEEDED). Not always present.

field.description: The request field that caused the error, using dot notation for nested fields (e.g., 'guest.email'). Only present for validation errors.

errors.description: Array of error objects describing what went wrong. Multiple errors may be returned for batch validation failures (e.g., multiple invalid fields in a request body).

requestid.description: Unique identifier for this request, generated server-side. Include this value when contacting support about an error. Also returned in the X-Request-Id response header.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response format returned by all FreeRez API endpoints when a request fails. Contains one or more error objects with descriptive messages and a request identifier for support reference.

id.description: Unique tenant identifier (UUID v4). Used in admin API paths and API client associations.

name.description: Human-readable display name of the tenant organization (e.g., 'Downtown Dining Group'). Shown in admin interfaces.

slug.description: URL-safe unique identifier for the tenant. Lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens. Used in routing and database naming.

email.description: Primary contact email for the tenant owner. Receives onboarding communications, billing notifications, and critical platform alerts.

plan.description: Subscription plan determining feature access, rate limit multipliers, and support tier. Plans: free (evaluation), starter (single restaurant), professional (multi-restaurant), enterprise (custom).

maxRestaurants.description: Maximum number of restaurants this tenant can create. Enforced at restaurant creation time. Contact support to increase.

status.description: Current tenant lifecycle state. 'active': fully operational. 'suspended': API access disabled (typically for billing). 'cancelled': soft-deleted, pending permanent removal after 30 days.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the tenant was initially created on the platform.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification to the tenant record (plan change, status change, etc.).

Tenant.description: Represents a tenant (restaurant group or organization) on the FreeRez platform. Tenants are the top-level organizational unit with isolated databases and configurable plans.

id.description: Internal auto-increment ID for the tenant-restaurant association record.

tenantId.description: UUID of the tenant that owns this restaurant.

rid.description: Numeric restaurant identifier used throughout the API for all restaurant-scoped operations. Globally unique across all tenants.

restaurantName.description: Display name of the restaurant at the time of creation. May differ from the current profile name if updated later.

active.description: Whether the restaurant is active and accessible via the API. Inactive restaurants are hidden from all non-admin endpoints.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this restaurant was added to the tenant.

TenantRestaurant.description: Represents the association between a tenant and a restaurant. Links a restaurant's numeric ID (rid) to its owning tenant organization.

id.description: Internal auto-increment ID for the client record.

tenantId.description: UUID of the tenant this client belongs to. Determines which database the client's requests are routed to.

clientId.description: The client's public identifier, prefixed with 'fr_'. Used as the username in OAuth client credentials flow (HTTP Basic Auth).

name.description: Human-readable label for the client (e.g., 'Production Booking Widget', 'POS Integration'). For administrative identification only.

partnerId.description: Optional partner identifier for tracking which integration partner owns this client. Used for webhook scoping and analytics.

scope.description: OAuth scope granted to tokens issued to this client. Currently always 'DEFAULT'. Reserved for future fine-grained permission scoping.

tier.description: Data access tier controlling which fields are visible in API responses. 'copper': basic (no PII), 'gold': enhanced (+ guest PII), 'platinum': full (+ marketing/campaign data).

allowedRids.description: Array of restaurant IDs this client is authorized to access. Empty array or null means unrestricted access to all restaurants in the tenant. Non-empty array restricts to only those restaurants.

active.description: Whether the client can obtain new OAuth tokens. Setting to false prevents new authentication but does not immediately invalidate existing tokens.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the client was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification to the client record.

ApiClient.description: Represents an API client (credential pair) that can authenticate to the FreeRez API. Clients belong to a tenant and may be restricted to specific restaurants.

rid.description: Numeric restaurant identifier used in all API path parameters. Globally unique and stable.

name.description: Restaurant display name as configured in its profile.

address.description: Primary street address line (e.g., '123 Main Street').

address2.description: Secondary address line (suite, floor, unit). May be empty.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude as a string decimal (e.g., '40.7128'). Used for map display and proximity searches.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude as a string decimal (e.g., '-74.0060'). Used for map display and proximity searches.

postal_code.description: Postal or ZIP code for the restaurant's address.

phone_number.description: Primary contact phone number for the restaurant. Format varies by country.

metro_name.description: Metropolitan area name for grouping restaurants by region (e.g., 'New York', 'San Francisco Bay Area').

reservation_url.description: Parametric URL for the restaurant's reservation page. Suitable for programmatic linking.

profile_url.description: Parametric URL for the restaurant's public profile page.

natural_reservation_url.description: SEO-friendly reservation URL using the restaurant's name slug. Preferred for user-facing links.

natural_profile_url.description: SEO-friendly profile URL using the restaurant's name slug. Preferred for user-facing links.

profile_photo.description: Object containing the restaurant's primary profile photo URL and dimensions. May be null if no photo is set.

RestaurantListItem.description: Summary representation of a restaurant as returned in list/directory endpoints. Contains essential display and location information.

id.description: Restaurant identifier. Should match the rid in the URL path. Validated for consistency.

name.description: Updated display name for the restaurant.

primaryCuisine.description: Primary cuisine type (e.g., 'Italian', 'Japanese', 'American'). Used for search and categorization.

phone.description: Updated contact phone number for the restaurant.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the restaurant's pricing (e.g., 'USD', 'GBP', 'EUR').

locale.description: BCP 47 locale tag for the restaurant's primary language and region (e.g., 'en-US', 'fr-FR').

website.description: Restaurant's website URL. Must include protocol (https://).

timeZone.description: IANA timezone identifier for the restaurant's local time (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'). Critical for availability calculations.

description.description: Full-text description of the restaurant for profile pages. Supports basic markdown formatting. Max 5,000 characters.

diningStyle.description: Describes the dining atmosphere (e.g., 'Fine Dining', 'Casual Elegant', 'Casual Dining', 'Home Style').

dressCode.description: Suggested dress code for diners (e.g., 'Business Casual', 'Smart Casual', 'No Dress Code').

privateEventDetails.description: Description of private event offerings and capabilities. Displayed on the restaurant's private dining page.

cateringDetails.description: Description of catering services offered. Displayed on the restaurant's profile.

executiveChef.description: Name of the executive chef or head chef for the restaurant profile.

crossStreet.description: Nearest cross street or landmark for helping diners locate the restaurant (e.g., 'Between 5th and 6th Ave').

location.description: Geographic coordinates object with latitude and longitude fields for map positioning.

profileImage.description: Primary profile image. Can be a URL string or an object with url and dimensions.

images.description: Array of additional restaurant images (interior, food, exterior). Ordered for display; first image is featured.

tags.description: Array of descriptive tags for the restaurant (cuisine types, features, ambiance). Used for search and discovery.

openingTimes.description: Map of day names to time ranges

ProfileUpdateBody.description: Request body for updating a restaurant's public-facing profile. All fields are optional — include only those you want to change (partial update semantics).

rid.description: Restaurant identifier this availability data is for.

party_size.description: The requested party size that availability was calculated for. Availability varies by party size due to table configurations.

times.description: Array of all possible time slots in the restaurant's schedule for the requested date, regardless of availability. Use for displaying a complete schedule grid.

times_available.description: Array of time slots that have remaining capacity for the requested party size and date. Subset of the 'times' array. Use these to show bookable options to the diner.

AvailabilityResponse.description: Response from the availability query endpoint containing all time slots and indicating which have remaining capacity for the requested party size.

experience_id.description: Unique numeric identifier for this experience. Referenced in reservation creation and pricing endpoints.

version.description: Monotonically increasing version number. Increments when any detail (pricing, description, availability) changes. Use for cache invalidation.

name.description: Display name of the experience (e.g., '5-Course Tasting Menu', 'Saturday Brunch Prix Fixe').

description.description: Rich text description of the experience including courses, inclusions, and dietary options. May contain line breaks.

rid.description: Restaurant ID this experience belongs to.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for all pricing in this experience (e.g., 'USD').

bookable.description: Whether this experience can be booked online. If false, the experience is only available via phone or in-house booking.

type.description: Human-readable experience type label (e.g., 'Prix Fixe', 'Tasting Menu', 'Wine Pairing').

type_enum.description: Machine-readable type classification: prix_fixe, tasting, wine_pairing, event, chefs_table. Use for filtering and categorization.

type_id.description: Numeric type identifier for legacy compatibility.

price_info.description: Pricing details object containing base price per person, included items, and price range information.

add_ons.description: Available add-on supplements (e.g., wine pairing, extra course) with individual pricing. Each add-on has an ID, name, and price.

add_ons_summary.description: Summary of add-on pricing: count of available add-ons, price range (min/max), and formatted display strings.

Experience.description: Represents a bookable dining experience (prix-fixe menu, tasting menu, wine pairing, chef's table event) available at a restaurant.

tip_amount.description: Calculated tip/gratuity amount in smallest currency unit. Based on tip_percent applied to the subtotal.

tip_percent.description: Tip percentage applied (e.g., 20 for 20%). May be mandatory based on restaurant configuration.

tip_amount_with_taxes.description: Total tip amount including any applicable taxes on gratuity (varies by jurisdiction).

service_fee_amount.description: Platform service fee in smallest currency unit. This is a FreeRez platform charge, not a restaurant charge.

add_on_subtotal_amount_with_taxes.description: Total cost of all selected add-ons including their applicable taxes.

add_on_subtotal.description: Total cost of all selected add-ons before tax.

item_subtotal_amount.description: Base experience price multiplied by party size, including taxes. The core cost before add-ons, fees, and tips.

taxes_on_tip_amount.description: Tax amount calculated on the gratuity. Zero in most jurisdictions.

taxes_on_service_fees_amount.description: Tax amount calculated on the service fee.

taxes_amount.description: Total tax amount across all taxable components (items, add-ons, service fees).

total_amount.description: Grand total: items + add-ons + taxes + service fees + tip. The amount the diner will be charged.

taxes_on_item_amount.description: Tax amount calculated specifically on the base experience items.

taxes_on_add_ons_amount.description: Tax amount calculated specifically on selected add-ons.

item_subtotal_amount_without_taxes.description: Base experience price multiplied by party size, before any taxes are applied.

is_tip_mandatory.description: Whether gratuity is automatically included and cannot be removed by the diner.

subtotals_per_price_type.description: Array of subtotal breakdowns grouped by price category (e.g., food, beverage, merchandise). Useful for itemized receipts.

taxes.description: Array of individual tax line items with name, rate, and amount. Shows exactly which taxes are applied (e.g., sales tax, hospitality tax).

ExperienceTotalEntry.description: Detailed pricing breakdown for an experience calculation, including all components: item cost, add-ons, taxes, service fees, and gratuity. All monetary amounts are in the smallest currency denomination (e.g., cents).

ReservationFull.description: Complete reservation record containing all details: identity, scheduling, guest info, state, experience details, POS data, and staff notes. The full representation returned by get/create/update operations.

id.description: Unique reservation identifier (UUID). Used in API paths for single-reservation operations.

href.description: Self-referencing API URL for this reservation resource. Can be used for direct navigation.

rid.description: Restaurant ID this reservation belongs to.

sequence_id.description: Auto-incrementing sequence number within the restaurant. Useful for ordering and internal reference.

guest_id.description: UUID of the linked guest profile from the CRM. May be null for unlinked reservations.

guest.description: Href URL to the guest resource. Resolve to get full guest profile details.

confirmation_id.description: Short numeric confirmation code shown to the diner for phone/walk-in identification (e.g., 12345). Unique within the restaurant.

state.description: Current lifecycle state of the reservation: 'pending' (awaiting confirmation), 'confirmed' (booked), 'seated' (diner arrived), 'completed' (visit done), 'cancelled' (cancelled by diner/staff), 'no_show' (diner didn't arrive).

table_number.description: Assigned table number/name. Set by host-stand staff at seating time. May be null if not yet assigned.

created_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the reservation was initially created.

scheduled_time.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the reservation in the restaurant's local timezone. This is the time shown to the diner.

party_size.description: Number of guests in the dining party. Must be within the restaurant's configured min/max range.

visit_tags.description: Array of tag strings applied to this specific visit (e.g., 'birthday', 'anniversary', 'business'). Different from guest-level tags.

origin.description: Channel through which the reservation was created: 'online' (booking widget), 'phone', 'walkin', 'internal' (staff/API).

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification to this reservation.

seated_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the diner was marked as seated. Null until the state transitions to 'seated'.

done_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the visit was marked as completed. Null until state transitions to 'completed'.

pos_data.description: POS ticket reference data, populated when a POS ticket is pushed and linked to this reservation. Contains ticket ID and amount summary.

scheduled_time_utc.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the reservation in UTC. Use this for timezone-independent calculations and sorting.

guest_request.description: Free-text message from the diner submitted during booking (e.g., 'Window seat please', 'Celebrating our anniversary', 'Allergic to shellfish').

venue_notes.description: Internal notes from restaurant staff about this reservation. Not visible to the diner.

opentable_notes.description: System-generated notes or notes from the platform. Informational only.

table_category.description: Dining area or table category for this reservation (e.g., 'indoor', 'patio', 'bar', 'private room'). Set from availability selection.

server.description: Name or ID of the server/staff member assigned to this table. Set by host-stand at seating.

currency_code.description: ISO 4217 currency code applicable to any monetary amounts in this reservation (deposits, experience pricing).

currency_denominator.description: Denominator for currency conversion (e.g., 100 for USD cents→dollars). Divide monetary amounts by this value for display.

experience_details.description: Object containing attached experience information: experience ID, name, pricing summary, and selected add-ons. Null if no experience is attached.

deposit.description: Deposit information if the reservation requires a deposit: amount, status (pending/captured/refunded), and capture timestamp.

Guest.description: Guest profile record from the restaurant's CRM. Contains identity, contact info (tier-gated), tags, notes, and metadata. Represents a single diner's relationship with a specific restaurant.

id.description: Unique guest identifier (UUID) within the restaurant. Used in reservation linking and CRM operations.

href.description: Self-referencing API URL for this guest resource.

rid.description: Restaurant ID this guest profile belongs to. Guests are restaurant-scoped.

sequence_id.description: Auto-incrementing sequence number within the restaurant's guest database.

gpid.description: Global Person ID linking this guest to profiles at other restaurants within the same tenant. Enables cross-venue guest recognition and analytics.

tags.description: Array of tag objects applied to this guest (e.g., VIP, regular, dietary preferences). Tags are restaurant-defined.

birth_date.description: Guest's date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format. Used for birthday recognition. May be null if not provided.

anniversary_date.description: Guest's anniversary date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Used for special occasion recognition.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification to this guest profile.

forgotten.description: Whether a GDPR 'right to be forgotten' erasure request has been processed for this guest. If true, only the ID field remains; all other data has been purged.

archived.description: Whether the guest has been archived (hidden from default listings). Archived guests are retained for historical reservation data but excluded from active CRM operations.

company_name.description: Guest's company or organization name. Useful for business dining and corporate account tracking.

created_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this guest profile was initially created.

notes.description: General free-text notes about the guest. Visible to all staff. Used for miscellaneous information.

notes_special_relationship.description: Notes about the guest's relationship with the restaurant or staff (e.g., 'Owner's college roommate', 'Food critic for NYT').

notes_food_and_drink.description: Notes about food and drink preferences, allergies, and restrictions (e.g., 'Severe nut allergy', 'Prefers Burgundy wines').

notes_seating.description: Notes about seating preferences (e.g., 'Always requests booth 7', 'Wheelchair accessible seating required').

first_name.description: Guest's first name. Only populated for Gold+ tier API clients. Returns null/absent for Copper tier.

last_name.description: Guest's last name. Only populated for Gold+ tier API clients. Returns null/absent for Copper tier.

email.description: Guest's email address. Only populated for Gold+ tier API clients. Returns null/absent for Copper tier.

phone.description: Guest's phone number. Only populated for Gold+ tier API clients. Returns null/absent for Copper tier.

email_optin.description: Whether the guest has opted in to email marketing communications. Only populated for Gold+ tier API clients.

menu.description: Menu metadata object containing: id, name, description, currency, and ordinal (display order). Represents the menu itself (e.g., 'Dinner Menu').

groups.description: Array of menu groups/sections (e.g., 'Starters', 'Mains', 'Desserts'). Each group has an id, name, description, and ordinal for ordering.

items.description: Array of menu items across all groups. Each item includes: id, group_id (parent group), name, description, price (smallest denomination), and dietary flags.

MenuResult.description: Complete menu structure including metadata, groups (sections/categories), and individual items with pricing. Returned by the get-menu-by-ID endpoint.
